lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਝੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਡੁਬਾ ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: baajh guroo ddubaa sansaar |2|
lines.english: Without the Guru, the world is drowning. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਇਆ ਗਇਆ ਮੁਇਆ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aaeaa geaa mueaa naau |
lines.english: He came and he went, and now, even his name has died.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਅੰਧੁ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak manamukh andh piaar |
lines.english: O Nanak, the self-willed manmukhs love the darkness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਛੈ ਪਤਲਿ ਸਦਿਹੁ ਕਾਵ ॥
lines.translit: pichhai patal sadihu kaav |
lines.english: After he left, food was offered on leaves, and the birds were called to come and eat.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਸ ਬਾਲਤਣਿ ਬੀਸ ਰਵਣਿ ਤੀਸਾ ਕਾ ਸੁੰਦਰੁ ਕਹਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: das baalatan bees ravan teesaa kaa sundar kahaavai |
lines.english: At the age of ten, he is a child; at twenty, a youth, and at thirty, he is called handsome.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਲੀਸੀ ਪੁਰੁ ਹੋਇ ਪਚਾਸੀ ਪਗੁ ਖਿਸੈ ਸਠੀ ਕੇ ਬੋਢੇਪਾ ਆਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: chaaleesee pur hoe pachaasee pag khisai satthee ke bodtepaa aavai |
lines.english: At forty, he is full of life; at fifty, his foot slips, and at sixty, old age is upon him.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਵੈ ਕਾ ਸਿਹਜਾਸਣੀ ਮੂਲਿ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਅਪ ਬਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: navai kaa sihajaasanee mool na jaanai ap bal |
lines.english: At ninety, he lies in his bed, and he cannot understand his weakness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਰਿ ਕਾ ਮਤਿਹੀਣੁ ਅਸੀਹਾਂ ਕਾ ਵਿਉਹਾਰੁ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: satar kaa matiheen aseehaan kaa viauhaar na paavai |
lines.english: At seventy, he loses his intellect, and at eighty, he cannot perform his duties.

lines.gurmukhi: ਢੰਢੋਲਿਮੁ ਢੂਢਿਮੁ ਡਿਠੁ ਮੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਜਗੁ ਧੂਏ ਕਾ ਧਵਲਹਰੁ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: dtandtolim dtoodtim dditth mai naanak jag dhooe kaa dhavalahar |3|
lines.english: After seeking and searching for such a long time, O Nanak, I have seen that the world is just a mansion of smoke. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਕਰਤਾ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਅਗੰਮੁ ਹੈ ਆਪਿ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਉਪਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: toon karataa purakh agam hai aap srisatt upaatee |
lines.english: You, O Creator Lord, are Unfathomable. You Yourself created the Universe,

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੰਗ ਪਰੰਗ ਉਪਾਰਜਨਾ ਬਹੁ ਬਹੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਭਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: rang parang upaarajanaa bahu bahu bidh bhaatee |
lines.english: its colors, qualities and varieties, in so many ways and forms.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਆਵਹਿ ਇਕਿ ਜਾਹਿ ਉਠਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: eik aaveh ik jaeh utth bin naavai mar jaatee |
lines.english: Some come, and some arise and depart; but without the Name, all are bound to die.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਜਾਣਹਿ ਜਿਨਿ ਉਪਾਈਐ ਸਭੁ ਖੇਲੁ ਤੁਮਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: toon jaaneh jin upaaeeai sabh khel tumaatee |
lines.english: You created it, and You alone understand it. It is all Your Play.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਆਪੇ ਆਪਿ ਸੁਜਾਣੁ ਹੈ ਵਡ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਵਡਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: toon aape aap sujaan hai vadd purakh vaddaatee |
lines.english: You Yourself are All-knowing. O Lord, You are the Greatest of the Great!

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸੇਵਹੁ ਸਤਿ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੋ ਹਰਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਬਿਧਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: so sevahu sat niranjano har purakh bidhaatee |
lines.english: So serve the True and Pure Lord, the Supremely Powerful Architect of Destiny.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰੰਗਿ ਚਲੂਲਿਆ ਰੰਗਿ ਹਰਿ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh rang chalooliaa rang har rang raatee |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs are imbued with the deep crimson color of the poppy; they are dyed in the color of the Lord's Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਮਨਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਤੁਧੁ ਧਿਆਇਦੇ ਮੇਰੇ ਸਚਿਆ ਬਲਿ ਬਲਿ ਹਉ ਤਿਨ ਜਾਤੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jo man chit tudh dhiaaeide mere sachiaa bal bal hau tin jaatee |1|
lines.english: O my True Lord, I am a sacrifice, a humble sacrifice, to those who meditate on You within their conscious mind. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਖੀ ਦੇਖੈ ਜਿਹਵਾ ਬੋਲੈ ਕੰਨੀ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: akhee dekhai jihavaa bolai kanee surat samaae |
lines.english: With their eyes, they see, and with their tongues, they speak; with their ears, they bring the mind to awareness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਉ ਪਾਇ ਤਨੁ ਸਾਜਿਆ ਰਖਿਆ ਬਣਤ ਬਣਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jeeo paae tan saajiaa rakhiaa banat banaae |
lines.english: He placed the soul in the body which He had fashioned. He protects the Creation which He has created.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੈਰੀ ਚਲੈ ਹਥੀ ਕਰਣਾ ਦਿਤਾ ਪੈਨੈ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: pairee chalai hathee karanaa ditaa painai khaae |
lines.english: With their feet, they walk, and with their hands, they work; they wear and eat whatever is given.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਰਚਿ ਰਚਿਆ ਤਿਸਹਿ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਅੰਧਾ ਅੰਧੁ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jin rach rachiaa tiseh na jaanai andhaa andh kamaae |
lines.english: They do not know the One who created the Creation. The blind fools do their dark deeds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਭਜੈ ਤਾ ਠੀਕਰੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਘਾੜਤ ਘੜੀ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jaa bhajai taa ttheekar hovai ghaarrat gharree na jaae |
lines.english: When the pitcher of the body breaks and shatters into pieces, it cannot be re-created again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਹਿ ਪਤਿ ਪਤਿ ਵਿਣੁ ਪਾਰਿ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak gur bin naeh pat pat vin paar na paae |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the Guru, there is no honor; without honor, no one is carried across. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮਤਿ ਚਤੁਰਾਈ ਤਾ ਕੀ ਕਿਆ ਕਰਿ ਆਖਿ ਵਖਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: surat mat chaturaaee taa kee kiaa kar aakh vakhaaneeai |
lines.english: What can anyone say about their intelligence, their understanding or their cleverness?

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਂਦੇ ਥਾਵਹੁ ਦਿਤਾ ਚੰਗਾ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਐਸਾ ਜਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: dende thaavahu ditaa changaa manamukh aisaa jaaneeai |
lines.english: They prefer the gift, instead of the Giver; such is the way of the self-willed manmukhs.

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਹਿ ਕੈ ਕਰਮ ਕਮਾਵੈ ਸੋ ਚਹੁ ਕੁੰਡੀ ਜਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: antar beh kai karam kamaavai so chahu kunddee jaaneeai |
lines.english: The deeds which one commits, while sitting in one's own home, are known far and wide, in the four directions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਧਰਮੁ ਕਮਾਵੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਧਰਮ ਨਾਉ ਹੋਵੈ ਪਾਪਿ ਕਮਾਣੈ ਪਾਪੀ ਜਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jo dharam kamaavai tis dharam naau hovai paap kamaanai paapee jaaneeai |
lines.english: One who lives righteously is known as righteous; one who commits sins is known as a sinner.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਆਪੇ ਖੇਲ ਕਰਹਿ ਸਭਿ ਕਰਤੇ ਕਿਆ ਦੂਜਾ ਆਖਿ ਵਖਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: toon aape khel kareh sabh karate kiaa doojaa aakh vakhaaneeai |
lines.english: You Yourself enact the entire play, O Creator. Why should we speak of any other?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਚਰੁ ਤੇਰੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਤਿਚਰੁ ਜੋਤੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਤੂੰ ਬੋਲਹਿ ਵਿਣੁ ਜੋਤੀ ਕੋਈ ਕਿਛੁ ਕਰਿਹੁ ਦਿਖਾ ਸਿਆਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jichar teree jot tichar jotee vich toon boleh vin jotee koee kichh karihu dikhaa siaaneeai |
lines.english: As long as Your Light is within the body, You speak through that Light. Without Your Light, who can do anything? Show me any such cleverness!

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਦਰੀ ਆਇਆ ਹਰਿ ਇਕੋ ਸੁਘੜੁ ਸੁਜਾਣੀਐ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh nadaree aaeaa har iko sugharr sujaaneeai |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Lord alone is Perfect and All-knowing; He is revealed to the Gurmukh. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਆਪੇ ਜਗਤੁ ਉਪਾਇ ਕੈ ਤੁਧੁ ਆਪੇ ਧੰਧੈ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: tudh aape jagat upaae kai tudh aape dhandhai laaeaa |
lines.english: You Yourself created the world, and You Yourself put it to work.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹ ਠਗਉਲੀ ਪਾਇ ਕੈ ਤੁਧੁ ਆਪਹੁ ਜਗਤੁ ਖੁਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: moh tthgaulee paae kai tudh aapahu jagat khuaaeaa |
lines.english: Administering the drug of emotional attachment, You Yourself have led the world astray.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸਨਾ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਅਗਨਿ ਹੈ ਨਹ ਤਿਪਤੈ ਭੁਖਾ ਤਿਹਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: tisanaa andar agan hai neh tipatai bhukhaa tihaaeaa |
lines.english: The fire of desire is deep within; unsatisfied, people remain hungry and thirsty.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮੋਹੁ ਨ ਤੁਟਈ ਸਭਿ ਥਕੇ ਕਰਮ ਕਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur mohu na tuttee sabh thake karam kamaaeaa |
lines.english: Without the True Guru, emotional attachment is not broken. All have grown weary of performing empty rituals.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਸਾ ਇਹੁ ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ਹੈ ਮਰਿ ਜੰਮੈ ਆਇਆ ਜਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sahasaa ihu sansaar hai mar jamai aaeaa jaaeaa |
lines.english: This world is an illusion; it dies and it is re-born-it comes and it goes in reincarnation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਈਐ ਸੁਖਿ ਰਜਾ ਜਾ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramatee naam dhiaaeeai sukh rajaa jaa tudh bhaaeaa |
lines.english: Those who follow the Guru's Teachings meditate on the Naam, the Name of the Lord. Filled with a joyful peace, they surrender to Your Will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਲੁ ਉਧਾਰੇ ਆਪਣਾ ਧੰਨੁ ਜਣੇਦੀ ਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kul udhaare aapanaa dhan janedee maaeaa |
lines.english: They save their families and ancestors; blessed are the mothers who gave birth to them.
